title: Additional Permissions for Workspace Structure
excerpt: Currently we have permissions to manage a lot of things, such as preventing users from creating private boards, or preventing users from deleting items they didn’t create, etc. However, it feels like we’re missing a few …

posts.author: sealv_wsm (Daniel Alvarado)
posts.cookedHtml:
Currently we have permissions to manage a lot of things, such as preventing users from creating private boards, or preventing users from deleting items they didn’t create, etc. However, it feels like we’re missing a few key ones IMO:

  1. Prevent users from creating folders.

I’ve managed to prevent our “basic users” from creating boards, but they CAN in fact, create folders - which seems weird.

  1. Prevent users from moving boards between folders.

Similar to the above, some new users felt it was a good idea to start re-arranging items between existing folders - and the fact that I can’t prevent it is annoying.

If anyone knows a workaround, let me know.

Thanks!
